title: requests — model traits
description: One trait: the `IsAccessRequestable` marker. Composed as an OPTIONAL alternative to the `#[AccessRequestable]` PHP attribute for policy authors who prefer trait composition. The trait itself carries no behaviour — boot-time discovery accepts either signal into the same `AccessRequestableRegistry` map. This module ALSO consumes traits from foundation / tenancy / user / access/rbac for the read + join paths, but composes none of them on its own tables (there are no local tables).

owned.description: Marker trait — composed on any Eloquent model that can be the SUBJECT of an access request. Zero runtime behaviour; discovery walks every model in the classmap, unions those composing this trait with those carrying `#[AccessRequestable]`, and populates the registry with the union set. Prefer the attribute (it lets you declare the permission set inline); use the trait when a model already carries an attribute-heavy class docblock and you want to keep signals separated.

methods.name: accessRequestPermissions
methods.description: Consumers implementing the trait override this static to declare the permissions a caller can legitimately request. Default implementation returns [] (which means the model appears in the registry BUT no permissions can be requested — caught by the `permission_belongs_to_resource_type` rule). When the model carries `#[AccessRequestable(permissions: [...])]` too, the attribute wins.

methods.name: accessRequestResourceType
methods.description: Optional override for the registry key. Defaults to `Str::snake(class_basename(static::class))`. When the model carries `#[AccessRequestable(resourceType: '...')]`, the attribute wins.

appliedBy: sports::Athlete (planned; guardian relationships need this substrate)
region::Region (planned; enterprise tenants split reporting per Region)
notAppliedBy: Every platform-admin surface — Academorix staff use impersonation via access/delegation, not the access-request loop.
Root tenancy surfaces (`Application`, `Tenant`) — access is granted via user provisioning + invitations, not the request loop.
System-of-record substrate rows (audit rows, activity rows) — access is granted via tenant-admin permission on the substrate, never per-row.

consumed.description: Consumed indirectly — the underlying approval_instance carries application_id and the DTO surfaces it. This module reads via that trait's global scope on `workflow/approvals::ApprovalInstance`.
Consumed indirectly — same as above; approval_instance is tenant-scoped so every access-request read is auto-filtered.

compositionExample.note: A requestable model chooses ONE of the two idioms below. Both feed the same registry.

blueprintExample.note: The paired `->access_requestable()` macro is a REGISTER-ONLY annotation on the target's migration — it adds NO columns because access-request state does not live on the target's table. It exists so CI cross-checks can flag a table whose model claims to be requestable but hasn't been marked in the migration diff.
